serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a full 24 cm martabak manis cut into 10 pieces, weighing approximately 500 g, typically shared among 2–4 people.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Manis (Indonesian Sweet Pancake) — estimated from USDA Foundation Foods flour-egg-sugar pancake base plus Ovomaltine Crunchy Spread product data and Milo powder nutritional panel
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy); Nestlé Milo nutritional panel; Wander AG Ovomaltine Crunchy Spread product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for martabak manis with Milo and Ovomaltine filling exists in HPB Foodo, USDA, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy plain sweet pancake base (USDA FDC NDB 18290, ~250 kcal/100g), (2) Ovomaltine Crunchy Spread label (Wander AG: ~530 kcal/100g, fat ~28g, carbs ~62g, protein ~7g), and (3) Nestlé Milo powder label (~385 kcal/100g). Weighted blend calculated for a 500g martabak: ~300g pancake base + ~150g Ovomaltine spread + ~50g Milo powder, yielding approximately 370 kcal/100g for the composite. Values are estimates and should be replaced with laboratory analysis when available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pancakes and sweet flour-based confections — analogous estimate based on plain pancake GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis with Milo and Ovomaltine filling. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on plain pancake GI values (GI ~67, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008) adjusted slightly downward to account for the fat content from Ovomaltine spread, which slows gastric emptying. Glycemic load per 500g serving = (65 × 250g carbohydrate) / 100 = 162.5. This is a high glycemic load per full serving. Values should be replaced with direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100g; ~1050 mg per 500g serving) and high caloric density
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 pieces and avoid adding salted toppings, as a full 500g serving provides approximately 1050 mg sodium, approaching 46% of the 2300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~28g sugar/100g; ~140g per full serving) and medium-high glycemic index (~65) resulting in a very high glycemic load per serving (~162.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this food, as a full 500g serving delivers approximately 140g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~162.5, which will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike; if consumed, limit to 1 piece and pair with a protein-rich food to blunt glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~8.8g/100g; ~44g per full serving), high sugar, and high caloric density (~1850 kcal per full serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only, as a full 500g serving provides approximately 44g of saturated fat — more than double the recommended daily limit of 20g — alongside high sugar and caloric load that contribute to dyslipidaemia and weight gain.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Milo Ovomaltine Crunch Martabak is an Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis) sold in Singapore by Smile Martabak at Funan. The dish consists of a thick, fluffy, pan-fried wheat-flour pancake batter cooked in a round cast-iron mould approximately 24 cm in diameter, then generously filled and topped with Ovomaltine Crunchy Spread — a Swiss malted chocolate hazelnut spread with a distinctive crunchy texture — and Milo chocolate malt powder. The finished pancake is cut into 10 pieces and weighs approximately 500g. The flavour profile is intensely sweet and chocolatey with a malty depth from both Milo and Ovomaltine, and a satisfying crunch from the Ovomaltine spread's crispy malt pieces. The texture is soft and spongy inside with a slightly crisp base. Martabak manis is a beloved Indonesian street food with origins in Arab-Indonesian culinary tradition; the Milo and Ovomaltine variant is a modern Singaporean-Indonesian fusion adaptation popular among younger consumers. Nutritionally, this is a high-calorie, high-sugar, high-saturated-fat dessert food. Per 100g it provides approximately 370 kcal, 50g carbohydrate, 28g sugar, 16.5g fat, and 8.8g saturated fat. A full 500g serving delivers approximately 1850 kcal, making it a significant caloric load. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), but the glycemic load per full serving is very high (~162.5), making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for regular consumption by individuals with diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It contains gluten, eggs, milk, and soy allergens.

confidence_reason: No direct laboratory analysis or approved-source database entry exists for this specific commercial product (Smile Martabak Milo Ovomaltine Crunch). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted blending of USDA SR Legacy pancake base data with Ovomaltine Crunchy Spread and Milo powder product label data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney plain pancake data. Confidence is low and values should be replaced with direct measurement or manufacturer-provided data when available.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Beverages' (input) to 'Desserts' — this is a sweet stuffed pancake, not a beverage. The input food_group appears to be a data entry error. 2. Nutrient values are estimated composites; no direct approved-source entry exists for this product. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 not used; estimated at 5.2g from pancake base USDA data), polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 1.5g, trans_fat_g estimated at 0.2g from butter component. 4. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source components and is not a significant nutrient in this food type. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source data. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source data. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source data. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source components. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source data. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source data. 12. The input cuisine_tag 'Coffee' appears to be a platform tagging artefact and is not reflected in this record. 13. Halal status assumed based on Smile Martabak's general market positioning in Singapore; not independently verified. 14. Serving size of 500g / 10 pieces is taken from the input description and treated as reliable. 15. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 250) / 100 = 162.5.
